name: Python
creator: Guido van Rossum
description: Python is a high-level, interpreted programming language prized for its readability and simplicity. It is widely used for web development, data analysis, automation, artificial intelligence, and much more. Its expansive ecosystem and supportive community make it a top choice for newcomers and professionals alike.
usedFor: Web development, data science, scripting, automation, AI, education.
funFact: Python was named after the British comedy group Monty Python, not the snake.

steps.title: Writing Your First Python Program
steps.description: Learn to run Python and write your first simple script using print statements.
steps.beginnerTip: Make sure you have Python installed and use an online editor or IDLE to practice.

steps.title: Variables and Data Types
steps.description: Explore how to store data in variables and understand basic data types.
steps.beginnerTip: Variable names are case sensitive and should be descriptive.

steps.title: Control Flow with If Statements
steps.description: Introduce decision-making in code using conditional statements.
steps.beginnerTip: Indentation matters in Python; be consistent (usually 4 spaces).

websites.title: Official Python Documentation
websites.description: Comprehensive and official reference for learning Python.

websites.title: Real Python Tutorials
websites.description: In-depth Python tutorials for every skill level.

name: JavaScript
creator: Brendan Eich
description: JavaScript is the programming language of the web, enabling interactive sites and dynamic user experiences. It is executed in browsers and is increasingly used on servers and in mobile development. Its flexibility and ubiquity have established it as a foundational skill for modern development.
usedFor: Web development, frontend engineering, backend (Node.js), apps.
funFact: JavaScript was created in just 10 days and was initially called Mocha.

steps.title: Hello World in the Browser
steps.description: Learn to write and run JavaScript in a browser using the console or a simple HTML file.
steps.beginnerTip: You can run JavaScript directly in your browser's developer tools console.

steps.title: Variables and Data Types
steps.description: Understand how to declare variables and explore basic data types like numbers and strings.
steps.beginnerTip: Use 'let' or 'const' instead of 'var' for better code clarity.

steps.title: Basic Conditionals
steps.description: Make decisions in code using if/else statements.
steps.beginnerTip: Remember JavaScript code blocks use curly braces { }.

websites.title: MDN JavaScript Guide
websites.description: Comprehensive guide to JavaScript on Mozilla Developer Network.

websites.title: JavaScript.info
websites.description: Modern, thorough JavaScript tutorials and explanations.

name: TypeScript
creator: Microsoft
description: TypeScript is a typed superset of JavaScript that adds optional static typing to the language. It helps developers write more reliable and maintainable code, particularly for large-scale applications. TypeScript code compiles to plain JavaScript, making it widely compatible.
usedFor: Web development, large frontend and backend projects, tooling.
funFact: TypeScript is open source and actively maintained by Microsoft, driven by the needs of major web frameworks.

steps.title: Hello TypeScript
steps.description: Set up TypeScript and write your first typed program.
steps.beginnerTip: Transpile TypeScript to JavaScript using the 'tsc' command.

steps.title: Defining Types
steps.description: Explore explicit variable types and function parameter types.
steps.beginnerTip: TypeScript helps you catch mistakes before running your code.

steps.title: Interfaces and Objects
steps.description: Define object shapes using interfaces to organize code.
steps.beginnerTip: Interfaces clarify the expected structure of your objects.

websites.title: TypeScript Documentation
websites.description: Official TypeScript documentation.

websites.title: TypeScript Deep Dive
websites.description: Extensive open-source TypeScript guide.

name: C
creator: Dennis Ritchie
description: C is a foundational compiled programming language that has powered software for decades. Renowned for its performance and control, it is the backbone of operating systems and embedded systems. C's simplicity and efficiency make it essential for low-level programming but require rigorous attention to detail.
usedFor: Operating systems, embedded systems, performance-critical software, system tools.
funFact: The UNIX operating system and the C language were developed alongside each other at Bell Labs.

steps.title: Your First C Program
steps.description: Learn the structure of a basic C program and compile/run it.
steps.beginnerTip: C requires compiling; use gcc or another compiler to turn your code into an executable.

steps.title: Variables and Data Types
steps.description: Declare variables and understand fixed data types.
steps.beginnerTip: You must declare the type of each variable before using it in C.

steps.title: Control Flow
steps.description: Make decisions in code using if statements.
steps.beginnerTip: Every statement in C ends with a semicolon.

websites.title: C Programming - GeeksforGeeks
websites.description: Detailed tutorials and resources for C programming.

websites.title: Learn-C.org
websites.description: Interactive C programming tutorials in the browser.

name: C++
creator: Bjarne Stroustrup
description: C++ is a powerful general-purpose language built on C, adding object-oriented and generic programming features. It is widely used for high-performance applications, game engines, and real-time systems. Mastery of C++ gives you granular control over memory and performance.
usedFor: Game engines, high-frequency trading, real-time systems, desktop applications.
funFact: C++ was originally called “C with Classes” before getting its current name.

steps.title: Basic C++ Program
steps.description: Write and run your first C++ application.
steps.beginnerTip: Compile C++ code with g++ and run the output file.

steps.title: Variables and Functions
steps.description: Declare variables, write simple functions, and understand namespaces.
steps.beginnerTip: Use std::cout for output instead of printf in C++.

steps.title: Classes and Objects
steps.description: Create and use a simple class.
steps.beginnerTip: C++ is case-sensitive and requires precise syntax, especially with classes.

websites.title: C++ Reference
websites.description: The go-to reference for standard C++ libraries and language features.

websites.title: LearnCpp.com
websites.description: Modern beginner-to-advanced C++ lessons.

name: C#
creator: Microsoft
description: C# is a modern, object-oriented programming language developed by Microsoft for building a wide range of applications on the .NET framework. It is particularly known for developing desktop software, enterprise solutions, and games with Unity.
usedFor: Desktop apps, web apps (ASP.NET), enterprise applications, game development (Unity).
funFact: C# is pronounced 'C sharp' in reference to the musical note.

steps.title: Hello, C# World
steps.description: Write and run your first C# console application.
steps.beginnerTip: C# code must be inside a class and the Main function serves as the entry point.

steps.title: Variables and Data Types
steps.description: Learn to declare variables and their data types.
steps.beginnerTip: Use 'var' for automatic type inference but know the underlying type.

steps.title: Methods and Classes
steps.description: Define methods inside a class to organize logic.
steps.beginnerTip: Always end C# statements with a semicolon.

websites.title: Microsoft C# Documentation
websites.description: Official documentation and tutorials from Microsoft.

websites.title: C# Station
websites.description: Free C# programming tutorials and resources.

name: Rust
creator: Graydon Hoare
description: Rust is a systems programming language that guarantees memory safety and concurrency without sacrificing performance. It has quickly gained a reputation for reliability and minimal runtime overhead, making it popular for both systems and application-level programming.
usedFor: Systems programming, command-line tools, web services, safety-critical apps.
funFact: Rust was voted the 'most loved programming language' on Stack Overflow's developer survey multiple years in a row.

steps.title: Rust Hello World
steps.description: Install Rust and write a hello world program.
steps.beginnerTip: Use rustup to install Rust and cargo to run your programs.

steps.title: Variables and Mutability
steps.description: Understand immutable and mutable variables.
steps.beginnerTip: Variables are immutable by default; use 'mut' if you need to change them.

steps.title: Ownership and Borrowing
steps.description: Learn about Rust's rules for ownership and safe memory usage.
steps.beginnerTip: Take your time with ownership and borrowing—they are unique to Rust.

websites.title: The Rust Programming Language
websites.description: The canonical Rust language book and beginner resource.

websites.title: Rust By Example
websites.description: Collection of runnable Rust examples.

name: Go
creator: Robert Griesemer, Rob Pike, Ken Thompson
description: Go (or Golang) is a fast, compiled language developed at Google for building reliable and efficient software. Its syntax is simple and its concurrency features make it excellent for modern, scalable backend systems.
usedFor: Web servers, networking tools, cloud infrastructure, microservices.
funFact: Go's mascot is a blue gopher, which has become an iconic symbol of the language.

steps.title: Go Hello World
steps.description: Write and run your first Go program.
steps.beginnerTip: Go requires a defined main package and function; run using 'go run filename.go'.

steps.title: Variables and Types
steps.description: Define variables and explore Go's concise syntax.
steps.beginnerTip: Use := for quick variable declarations and type inference.

steps.title: Functions and Packages
steps.description: Organize code into reusable functions and import standard packages.
steps.beginnerTip: Go encourages clear, simple function signatures.

websites.title: Go Tour
websites.description: Interactive introduction to Go fundamentals.

websites.title: Go by Example
websites.description: Collection of practical code examples in Go.

name: Java
creator: James Gosling
description: Java is a versatile, object-oriented language built for portability and scalability. It powers enterprise software, Android apps, and large systems. Its wide adoption and mature ecosystem make it a mainstay in professional software engineering.
usedFor: Enterprise applications, Android apps, backend systems.
funFact: Java was originally designed for interactive television, but digital cable TV was not ready for it.

steps.title: Java Hello World
steps.description: Write and run your first Java program.
steps.beginnerTip: The file name must match the public class name.

steps.title: Variables and Data Types
steps.description: Declare variables and learn about Java's static typing.
steps.beginnerTip: Java variable types must be declared explicitly.

steps.title: Methods and Classes
steps.description: Understand how to write methods and create classes.
steps.beginnerTip: Methods must be inside classes in Java.

websites.title: Official Java Tutorials
websites.description: In-depth official Java learning resources.

websites.title: w3schools Java Tutorial
websites.description: Clear, step-by-step Java tutorials.

name: Assembly
creator: Kathleen Booth (early work), others
description: Assembly language represents the closest abstraction to machine code, allowing direct hardware manipulation. It is used when maximum performance or tiny code size are critical. Coding in assembly requires careful attention to computer architecture and memory.
usedFor: Embedded systems, device drivers, performance-critical code, reverse engineering.
funFact: The first assembler was written for the Electronic Delay Storage Automatic Calculator (EDSAC) in 1949.

steps.title: Hello, Assembly World
steps.description: Write and assemble a basic program that prints text (example in NASM for x86).
steps.example:
section .data
  msg db 'Hello, World!',0xA
section .text
global _start
_start:
  mov edx,13
  mov ecx,msg
  mov ebx,1
  mov eax,4
  int 0x80
  mov eax,1
  int 0x80
steps.beginnerTip: Assembly syntax varies between processors and assemblers.

steps.title: Registers and Memory
steps.description: Manipulate data using CPU registers and memory addresses.
steps.example:
mov eax, 5
mov ebx, 2
add eax, ebx
steps.beginnerTip: Always check which registers your architecture provides.

steps.title: Branching and Loops
steps.description: Implement simple branching using jump instructions.
steps.beginnerTip: Assembly requires careful planning of program flow.

websites.title: PC Assembly Language
websites.description: Free and comprehensive guide to Assembly programming.

websites.title: TutorialsPoint Assembly
websites.description: Beginner-friendly Assembly language tutorials.

name: BASIC
creator: John G. Kemeny & Thomas E. Kurtz
description: BASIC was designed to be an easy-to-learn language for beginners and non-scientists. Its simple syntax and command structure made computing accessible in schools and on early personal computers.
usedFor: Education, prototyping, simple business programs.
funFact: BASIC stands for Beginner's All-purpose Symbolic Instruction Code.

steps.title: Printing Your First Statement
steps.description: Use the PRINT command to display text on the screen.
steps.beginnerTip: Statement line numbers are optional in modern BASIC.

steps.title: Variables and Input
steps.description: Store and display data entered by the user.
steps.example:
INPUT "What is your name? ", N$
PRINT "Hello, " + N$
steps.beginnerTip: String variables often have a $ at the end in classic BASIC.

steps.title: Branches and Loops
steps.description: Direct the flow of your program using IF and FOR statements.
steps.example:
FOR I = 1 TO 5
PRINT I
NEXT I
steps.beginnerTip: Indentation helps readability, though not required.

websites.title: Vintage BASIC Programming
websites.description: Classic documentation and information about BASIC.

websites.title: BASIC Programming - TutorialsPoint
websites.description: Introduction to the BASIC programming language.

name: Lua
creator: Roberto Ierusalimschy, Luiz Henrique de Figueiredo, Waldemar Celes
description: Lua is a lightweight, fast scripting language designed for embedded systems and games. It is known for simple syntax, extensibility, and fast execution.
usedFor: Game scripting, embedded systems, configuration, robotics.
funFact: Lua means 'moon' in Portuguese, reflecting its Brazilian roots.

steps.title: Print Text to the Screen
steps.description: Use the print function to display information.
steps.beginnerTip: Lua uses single or double quotes interchangeably.

steps.title: Variables and Data Types
steps.description: Store numbers, strings, or tables in variables.
steps.beginnerTip: Declare variables with 'local' for best practices.

steps.title: Loops and Branches
steps.description: Control your program with loops and if statements.
steps.beginnerTip: Always close if or loops with 'end'.

websites.title: Lua.org Official Homepage
websites.description: Official Lua reference manual.

websites.title: Learn Lua
websites.description: Interactive Lua tutorials.

name: Ruby
creator: Yukihiro Matsumoto
description: Ruby is a dynamic, open-source language focused on productivity and developer happiness. Its elegant syntax is natural to read and write, making it popular for web development.
usedFor: Web development, automation, scripting, DevOps.
funFact: Ruby was influenced by Perl, Smalltalk, Eiffel, Ada, and Lisp.

steps.title: Hello Ruby
steps.description: Write your first program outputting text.
steps.beginnerTip: Use 'puts' for output; no parentheses are required.

steps.title: Variables and Methods
steps.description: Store values and write simple methods.
steps.beginnerTip: Methods are defined using 'def' and closed with 'end'.

steps.title: Conditionals and Loops
steps.description: Control your program with if statements and loops.
steps.example:
if 5 > 3
  puts 'Five is greater than three.'
end
steps.beginnerTip: Blocks of code are delimited by 'end'.

websites.title: Ruby in Twenty Minutes
websites.description: A rapid introduction to Ruby from the official website.

websites.title: RubyMonk
websites.description: Interactive Ruby learning platform.

name: Swift
creator: Apple Inc.
description: Swift is Apple's modern language for iOS, macOS, and more. It is fast, safe, and expressive, designed both for beginners and professionals building cutting-edge apps.
usedFor: iOS apps, macOS software, Apple ecosystem development.
funFact: Swift was announced at Apple's 2014 Worldwide Developers Conference as a successor to Objective-C.

steps.title: Swift Playground
steps.description: Run and experiment with code in Swift Playgrounds.
steps.beginnerTip: Use Playgrounds on Mac or iPad for interactive learning.

steps.title: Variables and Types
steps.description: Declare constants/variables and understand types.
steps.beginnerTip: let creates an immutable constant; var creates a mutable variable.

steps.title: Functions and Optionals
steps.description: Define and call simple functions; learn basics of optionals.
steps.beginnerTip: Functions need a name, parameters, and curly braces.

websites.title: Swift.org Documentation
websites.description: The official Swift language documentation.

websites.title: Hacking with Swift
websites.description: Extensive Swift tutorials and articles.

name: Kotlin
creator: JetBrains
description: Kotlin is a modern, concise language designed for JVM, Android, and beyond. It interoperates with Java and is now the preferred language for Android application development.
usedFor: Android apps, JVM applications, web backend.
funFact: Kotlin is named after an island near St. Petersburg, Russia, home to JetBrains.

steps.title: Hello Kotlin
steps.description: Create and run your first Kotlin application.
steps.beginnerTip: Kotlin scripts can be run from the command line or with an online IDE.

steps.title: Variables and Null Safety
steps.description: Use var/val for variables and learn about null safety.
steps.example:
val language = "Kotlin"
var version: Int? = null
steps.beginnerTip: Start with 'val' for safety unless mutation is needed.

steps.title: Functions and Classes
steps.description: Write simple functions and define classes.
steps.example:
class Person(val name: String)
fun greet(p: Person) = println("Hi, ${p.name}")
steps.beginnerTip: Kotlin favors concise, expressive code. Use templates (${var}) for strings.

websites.title: Kotlin Lang Documentation
websites.description: Official, complete Kotlin docs.

websites.title: Kotlin Bootcamp for Programmers
websites.description: Free beginner-friendly Android course with Kotlin.

name: PHP
creator: Rasmus Lerdorf
description: PHP is a server-side scripting language primarily used for web development. It's easy to start with and powers many content management systems, including WordPress.
usedFor: Web development, content management, server scripting.
funFact: The acronym PHP originally stood for Personal Home Page.

steps.title: Print to the Web
steps.description: Embed PHP in HTML and output content to a browser.
steps.beginnerTip: PHP code must start with <?php and end with ?>.

steps.title: Variables and Types
steps.description: Assign values and understand dynamic typing.
steps.beginnerTip: Variables always begin with a $ sign in PHP.

steps.title: Conditionals and Loops
steps.description: Control program flow with if/else and loops.
steps.beginnerTip: PHP statements use semicolons at the end of lines.

websites.title: PHP Manual
websites.description: Official manual and function reference.

websites.title: w3schools PHP Tutorial
websites.description: Step-by-step PHP tutorials for all levels.

name: Bash
creator: Brian Fox
description: Bash is a popular Unix shell and command language, used for system scripting and automation. Through simple scripts and command-line utilities, it's a vital skill for developers and system administrators.
usedFor: Shell scripting, automation, DevOps, system administration.
funFact: Bash stands for 'Bourne Again SHell', a pun on its predecessor, the Bourne Shell (sh).

steps.title: Basic Bash Script
steps.description: Write and run your first Bash script.
steps.beginnerTip: Always start scripts with #!/bin/bash and give execute permissions using chmod +x.

steps.title: Variables and Arguments
steps.description: Store values in variables and access script arguments.
steps.beginnerTip: No spaces around the '=' when assigning variables.

steps.title: Loops and Conditionals
steps.description: Automate tasks with loops and if statements.
steps.example:
for file in *.txt; do
  echo $file
done
steps.beginnerTip: Use clear, descriptive variable names in scripts.

websites.title: GNU Bash Manual
websites.description: Comprehensive and official Bash documentation.

websites.title: ShellCheck Online
websites.description: Lint and check your Bash scripts for common issues.

name: R
creator: Ross Ihaka and Robert Gentleman
description: R is a language and environment made for statistical computing and graphics. It is favored by statisticians and data scientists for data analysis and visualization.
usedFor: Statistical computing, data analysis, machine learning, research.
funFact: R was named for its developers, Ross and Robert.

steps.title: First Plot and Calculation
steps.description: Run basic calculations and plot a simple chart.
steps.beginnerTip: Use RStudio or R GUI for interactive exploration.

steps.title: Variables and Data Frames
steps.description: Store and work with data using data frames.
steps.beginnerTip: <- is the assignment operator; use it instead of '=' for assignment.

steps.title: Simple Analysis and Plotting
steps.description: Perform basic analysis and create visualizations.
steps.beginnerTip: R's help pages can be opened using the ? command, e.g. ?mean.

websites.title: R for Data Science
websites.description: Comprehensive book for learning R and data science.

websites.title: RProject Official Manual
websites.description: Official R core documentation and manuals.

name: Dart
creator: Lars Bak and Kasper Lund
description: Dart is an object-oriented language by Google, used to build scalable and fast apps. Most notably, Dart powers the Flutter framework for cross-platform mobile development.
usedFor: Mobile apps (Flutter), web development, backend services.
funFact: Dart was introduced at the GOTO conference in Aarhus, Denmark in 2011.

steps.title: Print to Console
steps.description: Write and run your first Dart program to print text.
steps.beginnerTip: Use DartPad.dev for quick experimentation.

steps.title: Variables and Types
steps.description: Declare variables with type inference or explicit types.
steps.beginnerTip: Dart supports both explicit types and type inference with 'var'.

steps.title: Functions and Classes
steps.description: Organize code with functions and simple classes.
steps.beginnerTip: Classes and methods use curly braces or arrow notation for single expressions.

websites.title: Dart.dev Tutorials
websites.description: Official Dart tutorials and examples.

websites.title: DartPad
websites.description: Online interactive Dart coding environment.

name: Scala
creator: Martin Odersky
description: Scala combines object-oriented and functional programming in one concise, high-level language. It runs on the Java Virtual Machine and is often used for distributed data processing and scalable applications.
usedFor: Big data (Spark), back-end services, distributed systems.
funFact: Scala stands for 'scalable language' and is designed to grow with user needs.

steps.title: Hello Scala
steps.description: Write and run your first Scala application.
steps.beginnerTip: Scala files end with .scala and can be run in the REPL for interactive sessions.

steps.title: Variables and Immutability
steps.description: Learn to use val and var for immutable and mutable variables.
steps.beginnerTip: Use 'val' when possible to write safer, more predictable code.

steps.title: Functions and Collections
steps.description: Write simple functions and use Scala's powerful collection library.
steps.beginnerTip: Scala favors expressive, concise functions—practice functional patterns.

websites.title: Scala Documentation
websites.description: The official Scala guides and documentation.

websites.title: Rock the JVM Scala Beginner
websites.description: Beginner-friendly Scala video course.
